content: Comparative analysis of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM vs RND vs hand-designed curious agents) reveals that intrinsic reward stability scales with how much the agent's episodic memory architecture prioritizes unexpected, unlabeled exploratory traces. We can reframe general agent design as a meta-curiosity problem: the agent's core decision loop is implicitly selecting which exploration strategies to retain based on an intrinsic reward signal derived from how novel those strategies are to its memory system.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
